@graph.name: Mercedes-Benz models fitted with the M113 V8

mainEntity.name: How much does an M113 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M113 typically costs £2,500–£4,000 supply only [ESTIMATED], with variation by AMG or non-AMG spec. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on the model and labour rates.

mainEntity.name: Is the M113 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the M113 rates 4.5★ out of 5 based on specialist feedback and UK owner data [MB-INTERVIEW]. Its main age-related issues are oil leaks and ignition wear, not internal failures.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common M113 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Oil leaks from valve cover gaskets and breather covers are most common, typically appearing from 80,000–120,000 miles. Ignition coil failure and harmonic balancer rubber separation also occur with age.

mainEntity.name: Which Mercedes models use the M113?

acceptedAnswer.text: The M113 powers a wide range: C43/C55 AMG, E55 AMG, S430/S500/S55, SL500/SL55, CL500/CL55, ML430/ML500/ML55, G500/G55, CLK430/CLK55, and R500 — across late‑1990s to early‑2010s models.

mainEntity.name: How long does the M113 last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With regular oil changes and proactive gasket maintenance, the M113 routinely exceeds 200,000 miles. Many specialists report seeing examples at 250,000+ without internal rebuilds [MB-INTERVIEW].

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M113 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes M113 is a 4.3–5.5L naturally aspirated V8 — widely regarded as one of the most durable German V8s ever built. Backed by real UK enquiries in 2025 [MB-VERIFIED].

verdictRating.confidence: 90% confidence — based on UK enquiry data and cross-referenced specialist reports.

rows.score: 16 (Mix of cheap fixes (gaskets, coils) and moderate-cost items; mostly well-understood repairs)
20 (Parts cheap and available from multiple UK suppliers; high-volume V8 with strong aftermarket support)
16 (Reconditioned cost 30–44% of lowest host vehicle value)

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The M113 is one of Mercedes' most durable V8s — age-related oil leaks and ignition wear are its main weaknesses — buy on maintenance history, not mileage.
verdictRating.bestFor: Buyers wanting a bulletproof V8 Mercedes with reasonable running costs for the era.
verdictRating.avoidIf: You can't budget for proactive gasket and ignition system maintenance.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M113 typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED].
